title: Cleisostoma pensile
taxonomy: Cleisostoma pensile is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Cleisostoma is a part of the subtribe Aeridinae, which includes other orchid species. It is a terrestrial orchid, native to Asia. Cleisostoma pensile is a species of orchid that is relatively small in size. This species is often found growing in shaded areas and among tree roots.
uses: Cleisostoma pensile is a species of orchid used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Cleisostoma pensile is reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal applications. intro: Cleisostoma pensile is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its climbing habit and fragrant white flowers. Traditionally, Cleisostoma pensile is used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma pensile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cleisostoma pensile is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
